description: Complete guide to strings in C++: C-style strings (char arrays) and std::string. Covers declaration, input/output, common functions, manipulation, conversion, and best practices.

sections.title: Introduction to Strings
sections.definition: A string is a sequence of characters. C++ has two types: C-style strings (char arrays null-terminated) and std::string (modern, safer).

sections.title: C-Style Strings (Character Arrays)
sections.definition: C-strings are char arrays terminated by null character '\0'. Need to manage size manually. Functions in <cstring>.

sections.title: std::string Basics
sections.definition: std::string (from <string>) is the modern C++ way. Dynamic size, rich member functions, safe.

sections.title: std::string Access and Modification
sections.definition: Use [] or at() to access characters. at() does bounds checking. Modify with assignment, append, insert, replace.

sections.title: std::string Size and Capacity
sections.definition: size()/length() return number of characters. capacity() is allocated memory. resize() changes size.

sections.title: Searching and Substrings
sections.definition: find() returns index of first occurrence, rfind() from end. substr() extracts portion.

sections.title: Comparison and Conversion
sections.definition: Strings can be compared with ==, !=, <, >. Conversion between string and numbers.

sections.title: Iterating Over Strings
sections.definition: Range-based for loop, iterators, or index loop.

sections.title: Common String Algorithms
sections.definition: Reverse, palindrome check, count characters, tokenization.

sections.title: C-Style vs std::string Conversion
sections.definition: Convert between C-strings and std::string using c_str() and string constructor.
